ddls:
CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the machine */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the machine was installed */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine in the factory */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the machine belongs to */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the machine */,
  "production_line" TEXT /* Production line the machine is part of */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance activity */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., position, speed, rotation) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine the sensor is installed on */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "calibration_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the sensor needs to be calibrated */,
  "sensing_range" TEXT /* Sensing range of the sensor */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor the data is collected from */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data collection */,
  "axle_position" REAL /* Axle position value */,
  "speed_val" REAL /* Speed value */,
  "run1_rotation" REAL /* Run 1 rotation value */,
  "run1_cycle_end" INTEGER /* Run 1 cycle end indicator (0 or 1) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., good, bad, unknown) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine the maintenance is for */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  "cost" TEXT /* Cost of the maintenance activity */,
  "parts_replaced" TEXT /* List of parts replaced during the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Full name of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the technician belongs to */,
  "expertise" TEXT /* Area of expertise (e.g., electrical, mechanical) */,
  "certifications" TEXT /* Certifications held by the technician */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date of the last training session attended */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_technicians_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES users ("department")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from which the system was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work order */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine the work order is for */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician assigned to the work order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the work order was created */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the work order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the work order (e.g., open, in progress, completed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the work order */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the work order (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_orders_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_orders_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm event */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that triggered the alarm */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alarm event */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of the alarm (e.g., over-speed, over-temperature) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alarm (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alarm (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor maintenance record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor the maintenance is for */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  "cost" TEXT /* Cost of the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the alarm event that triggered the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of the notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alarm_id FOREIGN KEY ("alarm_id") REFERENCES alarms ("alarm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_downtime" (
  "downtime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each downtime record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine that was down */,
  "downtime_date" TEXT /* Date the machine was down */,
  "downtime_start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the downtime */,
  "downtime_end_time" TEXT /* End time of the downtime */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the downtime */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("downtime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_downtime_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "spare_parts" (
  "part_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each spare part */,
  "part_name" TEXT /* Name of the spare part */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the spare part */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the spare part in stock */,
  "unit_cost" TEXT /* Unit cost of the spare part */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supplier_info" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchase_orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase order */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the supplier the order was placed with */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the order */,
  "total_cost" TEXT /* Total cost of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchase_orders_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES supplier_info ("supplier_id")
)
